mission.title: Install and Configure Karmada on Kubernetes
mission.description: Karmada is an open-source multi-cloud, multi-cluster Kubernetes orchestration tool that enables seamless management of cloud-native applications across various Kubernetes clusters. Installing Karmada allows you to leverage its advanced scheduling capabilities and centralized management for improved application deployment and scaling.

steps.title: Create the Karmada Namespace
steps.description:
First, create a namespace for Karmada to operate in. Run the following command:

```bash
kubectl create namespace karmada-system
```

steps.title: Deploy Karmada Control Plane
steps.description:
Next, apply the Karmada control plane manifests. Use the following command:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/karmada-io/karmada/releases/download/v1.0.0/karmada-control-plane.yaml -n karmada-system
```

steps.title: Deploy Karmada Scheduler
steps.description:
Now, deploy the Karmada scheduler by applying the following manifest:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/karmada-io/karmada/releases/download/v1.0.0/karmada-scheduler.yaml -n karmada-system
```

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description:
To ensure that Karmada is installed correctly, check the status of the pods in the Karmada namespace:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n karmada-system
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Karmada
uninstall.description:
To uninstall Karmada, delete the namespace and all resources associated with it:

```bash
kubectl delete namespace karmada-system
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Karmada
upgrade.description:
To upgrade Karmada, apply the new version of the manifests. Replace `v1.0.0` with the desired version:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/karmada-io/karmada/releases/download/v1.1.0/karmada-control-plane.yaml -n karmada-system
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/karmada-io/karmada/releases/download/v1.1.0/karmada-scheduler.yaml -n karmada-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Karmada pods are not starting, check the logs of the pods for errors:

```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n karmada-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Karmada API Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Karmada API, ensure that the Karmada API server is running and check your network policies.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n karmada-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas Exceeded
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter resource quota issues, verify the resource limits set in your Kubernetes cluster and adjust them accordingly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Karmada will result in the Karmada operator running in the 'karmada-system' namespace with all necessary pods in a 'Running' state. You can access the Karmada dashboard if configured.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
